description_long: This house features a blend of traditional and modern architectural elements, with a prominent bay window section at the front and a large glass-walled extension at the rear. The exterior is clad in light-colored siding, and the structure includes both flat and sloped roof sections, with the rear extension topped by a slanted glass roof. The house has multiple stories, with visible interior rooms and furnishings, and a spacious fenced yard or garden area at the back. Windows are arranged in both symmetrical and asymmetrical patterns, with large picture windows providing ample natural light to the rear living spaces. The main entryway is understated, integrated into the front facade, and lacks a porch or overhang, emphasizing clean lines and simplicity. Overall, the design merges classic bay window charm with contemporary glass and open-plan features.
description: A two-story house with a bay window front, modern glass extension at the rear, and a large fenced yard. The exterior is light-colored with a mix of traditional and contemporary design elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the bay window section, main entryway, and part of the interior layout.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the sloped glass roof extension and the transition between traditional and modern sections.
description_view_2: Rear view dominated by the large glass wall and fenced yard, emphasizing the modern extension.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the flat and sloped roof junction, with the bay window visible at the far end.
attribute_window_arrangement: bay windows at front, large picture windows at rear, mixed symmetrical and asymmetrical placement
attribute_entryway_design: Simple integrated entryway with no porch or overhang, minimal decorative elements
